summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Discrete random variable'?

"Discrete random variable" means: A variable that takes a finite or countably infinite number of specific values, each with a definite probability. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Probability distribution (discrete)'?

"Probability distribution (discrete)" means: A table, formula, or graph giving every possible value of a discrete random variable and its associated probability. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Variance of a discrete random variable'?

"Variance of a discrete random variable" means: σ² = Σ[(x - μ)²·P(x)]; measures the expected squared deviation from the mean. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Linear transformation rules'?

"Linear transformation rules" means: For Y = a + bX: E(Y) = a + b·E(X) and Var(Y) = b²·Var(X); adding a constant shifts the mean but does not change spread. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Expected value E(X)'?

In Discrete Random Variables, "Expected value E(X)" refers to: The mean of a random variable: μ = Σ[x·P(x)]; represents the long-run average outcome over many repetitions.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: In Discrete Random Variables, which term matches: "The mean of a random variable: μ = Σ[x·P(x)]; represents the long-run average outcome over many repetitions"?

In Discrete Random Variables, this refers to "Expected value E(X)".
